title: Red Hat Bug Fix Advisory: pam bug fix and enhancement update
overview: An update for pam is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.
description: Pluggable Authentication Modules (PAM) provide a system to set up authentication policies without the need to recompile programs to handle authentication.Bug Fix(es) and Enhancement(s): [RHEL8] The third field, as documented in shadow(5) seems to behave wrongly: if empty, the password is considered expired. (JIRA:RHEL-70476)
affected_products: Red Hat Enterprise Linux for Power, little endian
Red Hat Enterprise Linux for IBM z Systems
Red Hat Enterprise Linux for ARM 64
Red Hat Enterprise Linux for x86_64 10 x86_64

fixes.description: [RHEL8] The third field, as documented in shadow(5) seems to behave wrongly: if empty, the password is considered expired.

full_text:
An update for pam is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.

Pluggable Authentication Modules (PAM) provide a system to set up authentication policies without the need to recompile programs to handle authentication.Bug Fix(es) and Enhancement(s): [RHEL8] The third field, as documented in shadow(5) seems to behave wrongly: if empty, the password is considered expired. (JIRA:RHEL-70476)
